role_summary: SNCC field organizer in the early-to-mid 1960s; her testimony clusters with the SNCC-allied-women / Northern-recruitment subgroup in this corpus's embedding.
ai_reading: Noonan's top semantic neighbors are Betty Garman Robinson (entry 7, count 98), Jennifer Lawson (entry 59), Gwendolyn Zoharah Simmons (entry 53), Dr. Cleveland Sellers (entry 22), and Junius Williams (entry 72). Robinson, Simmons, and Sellers all sit within the broader SNCC-organizer cohort the embedding has converged on across this catalog, but the closest pairing here is with Betty Garman Robinson, the SNCC Northern-support fundraiser whose own page documents the Skidmore-Berkeley-SDS path into Atlanta SNCC organizing. The cosine pattern suggests Noonan's testimony shares vocabulary with the SNCC women whose movement work was as much about Northern recruitment and student-network organizing as about Mississippi or Alabama fieldwork. Her axis positions are all small (within +/-0.34), placing her among the corpus's centrist voices alongside McDew (entry 16), Terry (entry 40), and Richardson (entry 49).
biographical_paragraph: Martha Prescod Norman Noonan was a SNCC field organizer in the early-to-mid 1960s; her oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 83 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing under multiple slug variants is pending in this iteration; SNCC Digital Gateway returned 404 for the spelling searched. The embedding pattern visible above is the strongest available signal for her movement placement.

sources.title: Martha Prescod Norman Noonan oral history
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)
